source.name: AP French Language and Culture Course and Exam Description (CED)

license.notes: No verbatim copying; objectives and descriptions are paraphrased for spoken practice.

description.en: Understands the main point and overall meaning.
Infers implied meaning, intent, or relationship from context.
Recognizes attitude, perspective, and culturally specific cues.

rubrics.notes: Designed for B1-B2 audio-first practice aligned to AP interpretive expectations without copying AP items.

description.en: Initiates, maintains, and closes the exchange; uses repair strategies.
Speech is generally understandable (pronunciation, rhythm, pacing).
Uses vocabulary and grammar with sufficient control for the task.
description.ko: 과제 수행에 충분한 수준의 어휘/문법 통제를 보인다.

description.en: Chooses register and politeness strategies appropriate to roles and context.
description.ko: 역할/상황에 맞는 레지스터와 공손 전략을 선택한다.

rubrics.notes: Focuses on spoken interpersonal communication (AP-style conversation) for Lingual practice.

description.en: Has a clear structure with transitions.
Develops a claim or comparison with relevant support.
description.ko: 관련 근거로 주장 또는 비교를 전개한다.

description.en: Uses vocabulary/grammar accurately enough to sustain meaning.
description.ko: 의미 전달이 유지될 만큼 어휘/문법을 정확히 사용한다.

description.en: Pronunciation, pacing, and intonation support comprehension.
Uses specific, non-stereotyped cultural examples and comparisons.
description.ko: 고정관념을 피하고 구체적 문화 예시/비교를 사용한다.

rubrics.notes: Supports cultural presentation (Unit 1) and cultural comparison (Units 2-3).

essentialQuestions.en: In Francophone contexts, what counts as family and how are family roles understood?
essentialQuestions.ko: 프랑스어권 맥락에서 '가족'은 어떻게 정의되고 역할은 어떻게 이해되는가?

essentialQuestions.en: What values and everyday practices shape family life across Francophone communities?
essentialQuestions.ko: 프랑스어권 공동체에서 가족 생활을 형성하는 가치/일상 관행은 무엇인가?

essentialQuestions.en: What pressures and changes do modern families experience, and how do people respond?
essentialQuestions.ko: 현대 사회에서 가족이 겪는 변화/압력은 무엇이며 사람들은 어떻게 대응하는가?

sourceRefs.locator: Course Framework V.1 - Unit 1 guide (themes/contexts, skill-building notes, Progress Check 1)
sourceRefs.notes: Used as non-verbatim alignment reference; Lingual focuses on spoken practice.

sourceRefs.locator: Progress Check 1 task models include: Email Reply (writing), Conversation (spoken interpersonal), Cultural Presentation (spoken presentational)
sourceRefs.notes: Writing task is preserved as a reference only (no writing objectives generated).

title.en: The Influence of Language and Culture on Identity

ap.title: The Influence of Language and Culture on Identity

essentialQuestions.en: How can identity shift over time, across places, and across communities?
essentialQuestions.ko: 정체성은 시간/장소/공동체에 따라 어떻게 변화할 수 있는가?

essentialQuestions.en: How does language choice (and accent) shape belonging and cultural identity?
essentialQuestions.ko: 언어 선택(및 억양)은 소속감과 문화적 정체성을 어떻게 형성하는가?

essentialQuestions.en: How do technology and media influence how we build and present identity?
essentialQuestions.ko: 기술과 미디어는 정체성을 형성/표현하는 방식에 어떤 영향을 주는가?

essentialQuestions.en: How can a community's art and media express its public identity?
essentialQuestions.ko: 공동체의 예술/미디어는 공적 정체성을 어떻게 드러낼 수 있는가?

sourceRefs.locator: Course Framework V.1 - Unit 2 guide (themes/contexts, skill-building notes, Progress Check 2)
sourceRefs.notes: Writing tasks are preserved only as source references; spoken tasks are prioritized.

sourceRefs.locator: Progress Check 2 task models include: Argumentative Essay (writing), Cultural Comparison (spoken presentational)
sourceRefs.notes: Writing task is preserved as a reference only (no writing objectives generated).

essentialQuestions.en: How do beauty ideals and aesthetics shape daily choices and social expectations?
essentialQuestions.ko: 미의 이상과 미학은 일상 선택과 사회적 기대를 어떻게 바꾸는가?

essentialQuestions.en: How can art reflect cultural perspectives - and also challenge them?
essentialQuestions.ko: 예술은 문화적 관점을 어떻게 반영하고, 동시에 어떻게 도전할 수 있는가?

essentialQuestions.en: Why do communities invest in beauty and art, and what do they gain from it?
essentialQuestions.ko: 공동체는 왜 미/예술을 가치화하며 그로부터 무엇을 얻는가?

essentialQuestions.en: How can the arts preserve, reinterpret, or document history?
essentialQuestions.ko: 예술은 역사를 어떻게 보존/재해석/기록할 수 있는가?

sourceRefs.locator: Course Framework V.1 - Unit 3 guide (themes/contexts, skill-building notes, Progress Check 3)
sourceRefs.notes: Cultural comparison is treated as spoken presentational practice with anti-stereotype guidance.

sourceRefs.locator: Progress Check 3 task models include: Email Reply (writing), Conversation (spoken interpersonal), Cultural Comparison (spoken presentational)
sourceRefs.notes: Writing task is preserved as a reference only (no writing objectives generated).

moduleGoal.en: Describe family structure and relationships, and handle basic back-and-forth questions about family life.
moduleGoal.ko: 가족 구성/관계를 묘사하고 가족 생활에 대한 기본 질문을 주고받을 수 있다.

seed.notes: A peer introduces their family and daily routines; some cultural cues appear in greetings/closings.
Light conversation about family members, routines, and values; practice follow-up questions and repair.
A 60-second cultural presentation about a family-related practice in a Francophone community.

comprehension.notes: Train quick extraction of relationship words and location cues in audio.

comprehensibility.notes: Prioritize being understood in short turns.
examples: Tu as des freres et soeurs?

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use relationship terms accurately (including step/blended family patterns).

language_control.notes: Support accurate descriptions in B1 speech.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 1 - Audio/audiovisual interpretive + conversation practice (non-verbatim alignment)

moduleGoal.en: Understand and discuss family traditions and celebrations, and coordinate invitations and plans politely.
moduleGoal.ko: 가족 전통/축하 행사를 이해하고, 초대/약속을 공손하게 조율할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Audio about an upcoming celebration; includes dates, times, and expectations.
Invite, accept/decline, propose alternatives, and confirm details; practice polite softeners.
A 60-second presentation describing a tradition and why it matters, with one concrete example.

comprehension.notes: Track dates, durations, and constraints in audio.

examples: On se retrouve vers huit heures.

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use event and planning vocabulary in context.

language_control.notes: Use simple past reference to talk about traditions.

cultural_awareness.notes: Match politeness and expectations to context.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 1 - Conversation practice + gradual build toward cultural comparison (via cultural presentation)

moduleGoal.en: Interpret discussions about family-related challenges and share opinions using basic comparison and cause-effect language.
moduleGoal.ko: 가족 관련 사회적 이슈를 이해하고, 비교/원인-결과 표현으로 의견을 말할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Audio references simple statistics; learner connects key numbers to the speaker's point.
Discuss a challenge and propose practical actions; practice hedging and repair.
Short talk presenting one issue, one cause, and one consequence, with a simple comparison.

comprehension.notes: Build visual literacy for audio-plus-visual tasks.

comprehensibility.notes: Slow down strategically on numbers and comparisons.

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use neutral, precise terms and avoid stereotypes.

communication_strategies.notes: Keep interaction cooperative while expressing stance.

cultural_awareness.notes: Use scope-matched comparisons and specific examples.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 1 - Chart/graph comprehension and culturally grounded interpretation (non-verbatim alignment)

moduleGoal.en: Understand viewpoints about language and identity and share personal experiences with appropriate register and repair moves.
moduleGoal.ko: 언어와 정체성에 대한 관점을 이해하고, 경험을 공유하며 레지스터/수습 전략을 사용할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Interview about accent and belonging; learner extracts perspective and supporting examples.
Share a language-learning or multilingual experience; practice repair and empathetic responses.
One-minute cultural comparison: language/identity issue in your community vs a Francophone community.

comprehension.notes: Track speaker stance and justification in audio.

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use neutral, nuanced vocabulary for identity talk.

language_control.notes: Sustain narrative with simple temporal markers.

cultural_awareness.notes: Use specific scope-matched communities for comparison.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 2 - Interpretive audio reports and perspective-taking as prep for presentational tasks

moduleGoal.en: Interpret multimedia viewpoints about online identity and discuss effects with structured opinions and softening strategies.
moduleGoal.ko: 온라인 정체성에 대한 다양한 관점을 이해하고, 완화 전략을 사용해 구조적으로 의견을 말할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Audio report referencing an online post/advert; learner identifies claim, evidence, and implied attitude.
Exchange opinions, agree/disagree, and soften; practice evidence-based claims and repair.
A short talk: claim -> reason -> example -> conclusion on how media shapes identity.

comprehension.notes: Hear the argument structure even when vocabulary is new.

comprehensibility.notes: Use softeners naturally while staying clear.

vocabulary_usage.notes: Prefer concrete terms (platforms, ads, profiles) over vague words.

language_control.notes: Build longer turns with simple structures.

cultural_awareness.notes: Connect register and message to audience and medium.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 2 - Multi-source tasks (audio + print/visual) and gradual build toward cultural comparison

moduleGoal.en: Interpret perspectives from interviews about community art/media and compare how communities present themselves.
moduleGoal.ko: 공동체 예술/미디어에 대한 인터뷰 관점을 이해하고, 공동체의 자기표현 방식을 비교할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Interview about a project representing community identity; learner summarizes and notes cultural references.
Ask for and confirm information about an event/exhibition; practice formal register and clarification.
One-minute cultural comparison: how public identity is reflected through art/media in two communities.

comprehension.notes: Capture why the speaker created the work and what it communicates.

language_control.notes: Support presentational comparison with simple explanatory frames.

cultural_awareness.notes: Compare community-to-community (city to city, school to school) to avoid overgeneralization.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 2 - Cultural comparison build-up and organization guidance (non-verbatim alignment)

moduleGoal.en: Interpret attitudes in audio about beauty/marketing and discuss recommendations and comparisons with polite nuance.
moduleGoal.ko: 미/광고에 대한 태도를 이해하고, 뉘앙스를 살려 추천/비교/의견을 말할 수 있다.

seed.notes: A review/commentary with implied attitude; learner identifies point and inference.
Recommend or discourage a product/service; compare options and hedge.
Two-minute cultural comparison: beauty ideals and their impact on daily life in two communities.

label.en: Infer attitude from word choice and tone

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use evaluation words with appropriate intensity.

language_control.notes: Support comparisons across options and cultures.

cultural_awareness.notes: Use concrete examples and acknowledge variation.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 3 - Prepare students for full 2-minute cultural comparison; build cohesive expressions

moduleGoal.en: Explain an artwork's perspective and discuss interpretations while managing disagreement and clarification.
moduleGoal.ko: 작품의 관점/의도를 설명하고 해석 차이를 조율하며 대화할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Audio guide introduces an artwork and its context; learner summarizes and infers intent.
Discuss what the work 'means'; ask clarifying questions, soften disagreement, and repair.
Present an artwork with a clear thesis about perspective, supported by two details.

label.en: Summarize and infer intent in art talk

comprehension.notes: Extract key points and implied message from audio guides/interviews.

comprehensibility.notes: Use intonation to signal uncertainty vs conviction.

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use descriptive language and personal reaction verbs.

language_control.notes: Support claims with concrete details from the work.

examples: Qu'est-ce que tu veux dire par...?

cultural_awareness.notes: Link artwork to cultural products/practices/perspectives without overclaiming.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 3 - Listening comprehension support: pause-and-check details/inferences (non-verbatim alignment)

title.en: Community value and art as history

moduleGoal.en: Interpret multi-speaker audio about community arts and deliver a balanced cultural comparison with appropriate scope.
moduleGoal.ko: 공동체 예술에 대한 다화자 오디오를 이해하고, 적절한 범위로 균형 있게 문화 비교 발표를 할 수 있다.

seed.notes: Two speakers plan an arts-related event; light background noise; learner tracks who-says-what.
Coordinate plans and exchange opinions about why the community values arts; manage disagreement politely.
Two-minute cultural comparison: how communities value art and how art records history; avoid stereotypes.

comprehensibility.notes: Use pacing and transitions to keep comprehension high.

vocabulary_usage.notes: Use precise terms for heritage and community institutions.

cultural_awareness.notes: Compare communities of similar scope and cite specific practices/products.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 3 - Guidance against sweeping generalizations in cultural comparison (non-verbatim alignment)

canDo.en: I can understand a short voice message about someone's family by identifying the main idea and key relationships.
canDo.ko: 가족 소개 음성 메시지의 핵심과 관계 정보를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 1 - Audio interpretive practice to support interpersonal speaking (non-verbatim)

canDo.en: I can infer basic attitudes and social cues (e.g., closings, politeness) in a family-related audio message.
canDo.ko: 가족 관련 오디오에서 태도/사회적 단서(마무리/공손함 등)를 추론할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can describe family members and routines and ask follow-up questions in an informal conversation.
canDo.ko: 비격식 대화에서 가족 구성/루틴을 묘사하고 후속 질문을 할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 1 - Conversation practice and communication strategies (non-verbatim)

canDo.en: I can use clarification and repair moves to keep a family-life conversation going when I miss or misunderstand something.
canDo.ko: 가족 대화 중 놓치거나 오해했을 때 확인/수습 표현으로 대화를 이어갈 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can give a structured 60-second cultural presentation about a family-related practice in a Francophone community.
canDo.ko: 프랑스어권 공동체의 가족 관련 관습을 60초로 구조 있게 소개할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: Unit 1 - Build toward cultural comparison via short cultural presentation (non-verbatim)

canDo.en: I can avoid overgeneralizations by defining a specific community and using one concrete example in a short presentation.
canDo.ko: 발표에서 특정 공동체를 정의하고 구체적 예시 1개로 일반화를 피할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand an informal audio about a celebration by identifying key logistics (time, place, expectations).
canDo.ko: 축하/행사 관련 오디오에서 시간/장소/기대 사항 등 핵심 정보를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can infer implied meaning (politeness, urgency, constraints) in an event-planning audio exchange.
canDo.ko: 행사 계획 오디오에서 공손함/급함/제약 같은 암시 의미를 추론할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can invite someone, respond appropriately, and confirm plans for a celebration in an informal exchange.
canDo.ko: 비격식 대화에서 초대하고 적절히 응답하며 일정을 확정할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can politely decline and propose alternatives while keeping the conversation friendly and clear.
canDo.ko: 공손하게 거절하고 대안을 제시하면서 대화를 우호적으로 유지할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can present a tradition or rite of passage in 60 seconds with a clear beginning, middle, and end.
canDo.ko: 전통/의례를 60초로 도입-전개-마무리 구조로 소개할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can explain why the tradition matters using one reason and one concrete example.
canDo.ko: 전통의 의미를 이유 1개와 구체적 예시 1개로 설명할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand an audio report about a family-related challenge by identifying the main claim and two supporting points.
canDo.ko: 가족 관련 이슈 오디오에서 핵심 주장과 근거 2가지를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can connect simple chart/number information to what the speakers say in an audio-plus-visual task.
canDo.ko: 오디오+시각 자료에서 수치/차트 정보를 발화 내용과 연결할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can discuss a social issue affecting families and propose a realistic solution in a friendly exchange.
canDo.ko: 가족에 영향을 주는 사회 이슈를 논의하고 현실적인 해결책을 제안할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can disagree respectfully and use repair strategies to keep a discussion productive.
canDo.ko: 정중하게 반박하고 수습 전략으로 생산적인 토론을 유지할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can deliver a short structured talk explaining one cause and one effect of a family-related issue.
canDo.ko: 가족 관련 이슈의 원인 1개와 결과 1개를 구조적으로 짧게 설명할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can use simple comparisons (more/less, similar/different) to add nuance in a short presentation.
canDo.ko: 짧은 발표에서 간단 비교 표현으로 뉘앙스를 더할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand an interview about language and identity by identifying the speaker's perspective and main reasons.
canDo.ko: 언어/정체성 인터뷰에서 관점과 핵심 이유를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can infer how examples in the audio connect to broader cultural topics (belonging, community, identity).
canDo.ko: 오디오의 예시가 소속감/공동체/정체성 같은 문화 주제와 연결되는 방식을 추론할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can share a personal language story (past to present) and ask about the other person's experience.
canDo.ko: 언어 경험을 과거-현재로 연결해 공유하고 상대 경험을 물을 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can clarify and repair tactfully when discussing accent or identity to avoid misunderstandings.
canDo.ko: 억양/정체성 주제에서 정중하게 확인/수습하여 오해를 줄일 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can deliver a one-minute cultural comparison about language and belonging between two specific communities.
canDo.ko: 두 특정 공동체를 대상으로 언어/소속감 주제 1분 문화 비교를 할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can keep the comparison balanced by defining each community and using parallel points.
canDo.ko: 각 공동체를 정의하고 병렬 포인트로 균형 있는 비교를 할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand an audio report about social media and self-image by identifying the claim and supporting details.
canDo.ko: 소셜미디어/자기이미지 오디오 리포트의 주장과 근거를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can infer the speaker's attitude toward advertising/online identity and connect it to cultural expectations.
canDo.ko: 광고/온라인 정체성에 대한 태도를 추론하고 문화적 기대와 연결할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can exchange opinions about online self-presentation and give one supporting example.
canDo.ko: 온라인 자기표현에 대해 의견을 교환하고 근거 예시 1개를 제시할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can disagree respectfully and soften my stance while keeping the exchange moving.
canDo.ko: 정중히 반박하고 완화 표현으로 대화를 이어갈 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can deliver a short opinion talk (claim-reason-example) about how media shapes identity.
canDo.ko: 미디어가 정체성에 미치는 영향을 주장-이유-예시로 짧게 발표할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can use transitions to keep my talk easy to follow and avoid absolute claims.
canDo.ko: 전환 표현으로 발표 흐름을 명확히 하고 단정적 표현을 피할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand an interview about community art/media by summarizing the project and its intended message.
canDo.ko: 공동체 예술/미디어 인터뷰의 프로젝트와 의도 메시지를 요약할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can pick out culturally specific references (places, institutions, practices) in an arts interview.
canDo.ko: 예술 인터뷰에서 장소/기관/관행 같은 문화적 단서를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can ask for and confirm event information (time, location, purpose) using an appropriate formal register.
canDo.ko: 격식 레지스터로 행사 정보(시간/장소/목적)를 요청하고 확인할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can politely request clarification and restate information to ensure mutual understanding in a formal exchange.
canDo.ko: 격식 대화에서 정중히 명료화를 요청하고 정보를 재진술해 상호 이해를 확보할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can give a one-minute cultural comparison about how public identity is expressed through art/media in two communities.
canDo.ko: 두 공동체에서 예술/미디어로 공적 정체성이 표현되는 방식을 1분 문화 비교로 말할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can choose scope-matched communities (e.g., city-to-city) to avoid stereotypes in a cultural comparison.
canDo.ko: 문화 비교에서 도시-도시처럼 동일 범위를 선택해 고정관념을 피할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand a short audio review about beauty or marketing by identifying the main point and key details.
canDo.ko: 미/마케팅 오디오 리뷰의 핵심과 주요 세부를 파악할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can infer implied attitude (approval, skepticism) and justify it with a cue from the audio.
canDo.ko: 오디오에서 암시 태도(찬성/회의)를 추론하고 단서로 근거를 댈 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can recommend or discourage a product/service related to beauty and give one reason.
canDo.ko: 미 관련 제품/서비스를 추천/비추천하고 이유 1개를 말할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can compare options and hedge my recommendation to match the other person's needs.
canDo.ko: 상대 필요에 맞게 옵션을 비교하고 완곡 추천을 할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can deliver a two-minute cultural comparison about beauty ideals and daily life in two communities.
canDo.ko: 두 공동체의 미의 기준과 일상 영향을 2분 문화 비교로 말할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can support my comparison with one specific example from each community, avoiding stereotypes.
canDo.ko: 고정관념 없이 각 공동체의 구체적 예시 1개씩으로 비교를 뒷받침할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand an audio guide about an artwork by summarizing the main points and context.
canDo.ko: 작품 오디오 가이드의 핵심 내용과 맥락을 요약할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can infer the artist's or curator's intent and point to one detail in the audio as evidence.
canDo.ko: 작가/큐레이터의 의도를 추론하고 오디오 단서 1개로 근거를 제시할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can discuss my interpretation of an artwork and ask clarifying questions about the other person's view.
canDo.ko: 작품 해석을 말하고 상대 관점을 명료화 질문으로 확인할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can soften disagreement and use repair strategies to keep an interpretation discussion respectful.
canDo.ko: 해석 토론에서 완화 반박과 수습 전략으로 존중하는 대화를 유지할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can present an artwork in 2 minutes with a thesis about perspective, supported by two concrete details.
canDo.ko: 관점에 대한 논지를 두 개의 구체적 근거로 2분 작품 소개 발표를 할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can use transitions and organization to make my presentation easy to follow for an audience.
canDo.ko: 전환/조직화로 청중에게 이해하기 쉬운 발표를 할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can understand a multi-speaker conversation about an arts event by tracking who says what and what is decided.
canDo.ko: 예술 행사 대화(다화자)에서 발화자별 정보와 결정 사항을 추적할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can infer each speaker's priorities and attitudes in a planning conversation even with light noise and overlap.
canDo.ko: 잡음/겹침이 있는 일정 대화에서 각 화자의 우선순위/태도를 추론할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can coordinate plans for an arts-related event and confirm details in a collaborative exchange.
canDo.ko: 예술 관련 모임을 계획하고 세부를 확인하며 협력적으로 대화할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can exchange opinions about why a community values the arts and reach partial consensus respectfully.
canDo.ko: 공동체가 예술을 가치화하는 이유에 대해 의견을 나누고 부분 합의를 이끌 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can deliver a two-minute cultural comparison on how communities value art and how art records history.
canDo.ko: 공동체가 예술을 가치화하는 방식과 예술의 역사 기록 기능을 2분 문화 비교로 말할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

canDo.en: I can avoid stereotypes by using scope-matched communities and specific cultural products/practices as support.
canDo.ko: 동일 범위 공동체와 구체적 문화 산출물/관행으로 고정관념을 피할 수 있다.

sourceRefs.locator: AP CED alignment reference (paraphrased; spoken-practice focused).

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Deliver a short listening-style source, then probe for gist, detail, and inference without front-loading the answers.

openingMoves: Present the source context and cue the learner to listen for the main idea first.
sustainMoves: Move from gist to supporting detail to inference questions while keeping each prompt anchored to the source.
closingMoves: Close by asking the learner to summarize what the source most strongly suggests.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must identify the gist, recover at least one supporting detail, and make a plausible inference.

promptCues: Keep prompts source-bound instead of turning them into general knowledge questions.
Escalate from broad comprehension to finer interpretation.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Simulate multiple related sources and make the learner compare how each source contributes to the meaning.

openingMoves: Frame the sources and cue the learner to identify what each source contributes.
sustainMoves: Prompt the learner to compare overlap, contrast, and usefulness across the sources.
closingMoves: Close by requiring a synthesis statement that combines the audio and visual evidence.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must compare multiple sources and synthesize their combined meaning.

promptCues: Ask the learner to cite which source supports the answer.
Do not let the learner stay at single-source recall.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Act as a cooperative conversation partner who guides the exchange while still making the learner carry the meaning-making load.

openingMoves: Open with a concrete personal or situational question that invites a direct response.
sustainMoves: Use follow-up questions, clarification prompts, and brief reactions to keep the exchange balanced and spontaneous.
closingMoves: Close only after the learner has responded, elaborated, and summarized or confirmed the main point.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must sustain the exchange across multiple turns and respond to follow-up prompts with meaningful elaboration.

promptCues: Keep turns short enough to invite learner output.
Prefer follow-up questions over long explanations.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Act as the AP-style conversation partner who speaks naturally, expects immediate response, and keeps the exchange moving under time pressure.

openingMoves: Start in character with a clear situational purpose and no extra setup lecture.
sustainMoves: Advance the conversation one prompt at a time, requiring the learner to react, ask, clarify, or negotiate in real time.
closingMoves: End after the learner has handled the final conversational move and clearly closed the interaction.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must complete the full simulated exchange, responding appropriately to each conversational move.

promptCues: Stay in role instead of switching into teacher explanation mode.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Act as a presentation coach who asks for a concise, audience-facing explanation anchored in cultural content.

openingMoves: Frame a clear audience and cultural focus before the learner begins.
sustainMoves: Prompt for organized explanation, supporting detail, and clear transitions without turning the task into conversation practice.
closingMoves: Close by requiring a final takeaway sentence that sounds presentation-ready.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must deliver a concise, organized cultural presentation with supporting detail.

promptCues: Favor structured explanation over turn-by-turn negotiation.
Keep the output concise enough for a short presentation.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Act as a presentation coach who prompts the learner to compare a target-culture practice with the learner's own or another known context.

openingMoves: Frame the two comparison points and ask for a clear comparison claim.
sustainMoves: Prompt for one similarity, one difference, and a brief explanation of why the contrast matters.
closingMoves: Close by asking for a concise concluding comparison statement.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must present a culturally grounded comparison that includes both similarity and difference.

promptCues: Push beyond list-like facts into explicit comparison language.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Act as a presentation coach who elicits a fuller cultural comparison with more support and development than the short version.

openingMoves: Frame the two contexts and ask for an organizing claim that will guide the comparison.
sustainMoves: Prompt for multiple supporting details, explanation, and clearer structure across the longer response.
closingMoves: Close with a synthesized conclusion that ties the comparison back to the broader cultural theme.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must sustain a longer comparison with multiple relevant details and a synthesized conclusion.

promptCues: Require more development than the 60-second variant.
Push for clearer organization and cultural interpretation.

activityTemplates.assistantRole: Act as a presentation coach who pushes the learner to take a position, support it, and present it coherently to an audience.

openingMoves: Frame the issue and require a clear claim rather than a vague reaction.
sustainMoves: Prompt for reasons, examples, counterpoint handling, and transitions that strengthen the speech.
closingMoves: Close by requiring a final persuasive takeaway sentence.
interactionPattern.completionRule: The learner must present a clear opinion with supporting reasons and a coherent closing.

promptCues: Push for justification and audience-facing clarity.
Do not accept an unsupported preference as complete.
